page: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a virtual private server belonging to the High Frequency Compute family. It features an Intel x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs, derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 4 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache, with support for AVX, AES, BMI2, and FMA instruction sets. The system is equipped with 2.0 GB of system memory, providing 1.0 GB of RAM per core, and includes a bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD for local storage alongside one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate average single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU scoring 1425.29 and single-threaded gzip compression reaching 24.24 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory-intensive operations show limitations. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance score of 2786.09 and the Passmark CPU score of 3137.23 fall into the weak category. Memory bandwidth is constrained, yielding 195.55 GB/sec for cached small blocks and dropping to 18.67 GB/sec read and 12.65 GB/sec write for large uncached blocks. Database and web serving metrics are also low, with Redis SET operations at 2.3 million operations per second and static web serving throughput at 2.3 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and low memory-to-core ratio, this server represents a budget-friendly tier designed for resource-constrained workloads rather than high-throughput production environments. The primary trade-off is the shared CPU architecture, which can introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads. Consequently, this configuration is best suited for lightweight applications, basic web serving, development environments, single-threaded utility scripts, or small-scale database testing where high memory density and dedicated CPU cores are not required.
description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a budget-tier virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, featuring an Intel x86_64 processor with 2 shared vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz. It is equipped with 2.0 GB of RAM, a bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD, and one complimentary public IPv4 address. Performance benchmarks reveal average single-core capabilities, such as a stress-ng single-core score of 1425.29 and gzip compression of 24.24 GB/sec, but multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak, with Passmark CPU scoring 3137.23 and uncached memory write bandwidth limited to 12.65 GB/sec. Due to its shared CPU allocation and low memory density, this server is a cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads. It is best suited for basic web serving, development environments, and single-threaded utility tasks.
og_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a budget-friendly virtual server featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 2.0 GB of RAM, and an 80 GB NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight workloads and development environments.
meta_description: Vultr vhf-2c-2gb is a shared-CPU virtual server with 2 vCPUs, 2.0 GB RAM, and 80 GB NVMe SSD, suitable for basic web serving and development.
tagline: A budget-friendly shared-CPU virtual server with NVMe storage, optimized for lightweight single-threaded tasks and development environments.
bullet_points: Intel x86_64 architecture with 2 shared vCPUs and 2.0 GB of RAM
Bundled 80 GB NVMe SSD local storage and 1 public IPv4 address
Average single-core performance suitable for basic single-threaded tasks
Constrained multi-core and memory bandwidth performance under heavy loads
Cost-efficient entry-point hosting for development and testing environments
